ddls:
CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department (e.g., sales, HR, IT) */,
  "department_head_id" INTEGER /* ID of the head of the department */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_types" (
  "job_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job type */,
  "job_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the job type (e.g., manager, developer) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_grades" (
  "salary_grade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary grade */,
  "salary_grade_name" TEXT /* Name of the salary grade (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "min_salary" REAL /* Minimum salary for the grade */,
  "max_salary" REAL /* Maximum salary for the grade */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "evaluations" (
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each evaluation */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being evaluated */,
  "evaluation_score" REAL /* Score of the evaluation (0-1) */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date of the evaluation */,
  "evaluator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the evaluator */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or feedback from the evaluation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("evaluation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "project_description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the project (e.g., active, completed, on hold) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assignment */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee assigned to the project */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date of the assignment */,
  "role_in_project" TEXT /* Role of the employee in the project */,
  "work_hours" INTEGER /* Total work hours spent on the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_project_assignments_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_accidents" (
  "accident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each accident */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee involved in the accident */,
  "accident_date" TEXT /* Date of the accident */,
  "accident_description" TEXT /* Description of the accident */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the accident (e.g., minor, major) */,
  "medical_attention" TEXT /* Whether medical attention was required (yes/no) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("accident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotions" (
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotion */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee promoted */,
  "promotion_date" TEXT /* Date of the promotion */,
  "new_position" TEXT /* New position of the employee */,
  "previous_position" TEXT /* Previous position of the employee */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the promotion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("promotion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_history" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "salary_date" TEXT /* Date of the salary change */,
  "salary_amount" REAL /* Amount of the salary */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the salary change (e.g., promotion, performance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_departments" (
  "employee_department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee department entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the employee joined the department */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Whether the department is the current one (1) or not (0) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_departments_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_job_types" (
  "employee_job_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee job type entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "job_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job type */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the employee joined the job type */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Whether the job type is the current one (1) or not (0) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_job_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_job_types_job_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_type_id") REFERENCES job_types ("job_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_salary_grades" (
  "employee_salary_grade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee salary grade entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "salary_grade_id" INTEGER /* ID of the salary grade */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the employee joined the salary grade */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Whether the salary grade is the current one (1) or not (0) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_salary_grade_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_salary_grades_salary_grade_id FOREIGN KEY ("salary_grade_id") REFERENCES salary_grades ("salary_grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR, manager, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user is active (1) or not (0) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user accessing the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
